tool.name: Compute genotype-based R²
tool.command:
mkdir -p r2_results_directory && mkdir -p temporary && mkdir -p vcf_tmp_preprocessing &&
bash '$__tool_directory__/VCF_statistics_linked_loci.sh' --input '${vcf_input}' --name '${vcf_input.element_identifier}' --max_SNPs '${max_SNPs}'

param.name: max_SNPs
param.label: Maximum number of SNPs per window
param.help: Maximum number of SNPs between the SNPs being tested for LD.

tool.help:
Usage =====
As input, users provide one VCF file (Danecek *et al.* 2011) and a window size (maximum number of SNPs between between any two tested variants).
If the VCF file contains missing values in the ID fields (.), the tool automatically generates a unique identifier using the following scheme: CHROM_POS_REF_ALT. This tool calculates the squared correlation coefficient between genotypes encoded as 0, 1 and 2 to represent the number of non-reference alleles in each individual. This implementation follows the standard LD measure used by PLINK.
The tool produces a tab-separated file containing the r2 values for each pair within the defined window.
**Example of output file**
::
CHROM POS1 POS2 N_IND R^2 1 23 25 51 0.0083333 1 23 77 52 0.0128144
See also examples of VCF formats in: https://samtools.github.io/hts-specs/VCFv4.5.pdf
